title: Customer Support Representative for Growing Women's Fashion Brand
description:
Dear candidate,



We run a fast-growing international women's fashion eCommerce brand. We currently operate one highly successful store and are actively planning to expand into multiple countries in the near future.



THE RAW REALITY OF THIS ROLE (PLEASE READ CAREFULLY):

Before you apply, we want to be completely transparent. We operate a dropshipping model, sourcing our products from China and shipping them to our main market. Because of the nature of this business, there are times when product quality or shipping times do not meet customer expectations.



You must be prepared to handle constant complaints and frustrated
s on a daily basis. Very often, the customer will be right, but your job will be to diplomatically defend the company's interests, protect our revenue, and negotiate partial refunds (you will receive performance bonuses for successfully achieving this).



Our previous employee could not handle the emotional toll of managing these complaints and resigned after just a few days. If you take angry
s personally or get overwhelmed easily, this job is not for you. However, if you are mentally resilient, thrive under pressure, and can handle difficult customers with grace, this is a massive opportunity. You will be stepping in as our right-hand person and first core tea
mber. You will grow alongside the business, and your loyalty and results will be highly rewarded with salary increases and bonuses.



MUST HAVE KNOWLEDGE OF THESE PLATFORMS:



Gorgias (support help desk)



Shopify (eCommerce platform)




 (internal team communication)



DeepL / AI Tools (for professional translation)



Responsibilities:



Handling customer inquiries and complaints with emotional detachment and professionalism.



Negotiating partial refunds and defending the company's bottom line.



Responding to customer
s regarding sizing, tracking numbers, and general FAQs.



Contributing to our documentation and improving our support processes.



Maintaining a strict ETA of 24 hours to clear the inbox.



We will provide you with:



Video training and clear Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).



Access to the Lead of Customer Support to answer all your questions.



Direct bonuses tied to your success in retaining revenue and successfully resolving disputes.



Applicant needs to:



Be available to join IMMEDIATELY.



Speak and write fluent English.



Have a minimum of 2 years of experience in Customer Support, ideally with prior dropshipping experience.



Possess a strong understanding of the Shopify environment. We highly value proactive problem-solvers who bring solutions to the table.



Be available for a minimum of 20 hours per week initially for a 2-week trial period. After that, if everything goes perfectly, you will be offered a full-time position.



MANDATORY TEST: As a final test of your dispute resolution skills and your ability to use AI translation tools effectively, please complete the following task at the very bottom of your proposal. Imagine an extremely angry customer writes to us in Spanish saying:



"¡Esto es una vergüenza! El abrigo que me ha llegado hoy es basura, no se parece en NADA a las fotos del anuncio, es plástico puro. Exijo devolverlo ahora mismo para que me devolváis todo mi dinero. Como no me deis una solución inmediata, voy a denunciaros por fraude y abriré un contracargo en mi banco."



Please write your exact proposed response to this customer in fluent, professional Spanish. Your goal is to de-escalate the situation, protect the company from the chargeback, and attempt a negotiation. Then, provide the English translation below it. Proposals without this exact test completed will be automatically rejected.



Please respond by telling me about your previous similar experience, confirming you have read the "Raw Reality" section, and explaining why you think you are mentally equipped for this job.

title: Customer Retention Specialist
description:
IMPORTANT: APPLICATIONS WILL ONLY BE CONSIDERED FROM CANDIDATES WITH PRIOR SAAS INDUSTRY EXPERIENCE IN RETENTION-FOCUSED ROLES.





ABOUT THE POSITION:

We are seeking a proactive and relationship-driven Customer Retention Specialist to support and nurture our growing customer base. In this role, you will focus on strengthening customer relationships, improving overall satisfaction, and promoting long-term retention.



The ideal candidate is highly organized, communicates clearly, and takes initiative in identifying ways to enhance the customer experience. You will work closely with customers to understand their goals, address concerns, and ensure they continue to receive value from our platform.



As the primary point of contact for assigned customers, you will build and maintain strong, long-term relationships while monitoring customer satisfaction and resolving concerns proactively. You will conduct regular check-ins to ensure customers are achieving their goals and identify opportunities to improve retention and engagement.



In addition, you will collaborate with internal teams to resolve customer issues effectively, assist with onboarding new customers, and maintain accurate records of all customer interactions. You will also track feedback, identify trends or recurring concerns, and support renewal efforts to help maintain long-term customer relationships.

title: Amazon PPC specialist (US Marketplace)
description:
We are Pediwise, a growing Amazon brand specialising in high-quality foot care products, primarily foot files. We sell in the US marketplace and are focused on profitable growth, efficient ad spend, and long-term brand building.



We are looking for a detail-orientated Amazon PPC specialist to manage and optimise our ad campaigns for maximum profitability.



Position Overview - 

This role is focused on:

-Lowering ACOS

-Increasing ROAS

-Improving keyword performance

-Scaling profitable campaigns

-Eliminating wasted ad spend



This is a long-term position for the right candidate.

Rate: $4–$6/hour (based on experience)

Hours: 5 hours per week (flexible, with potential to grow)



Responsibilities

-Manage Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, and Sponsored Display campaigns

-Perform keyword research and competitor research

-Optimise bids daily/weekly

-Add negative keywords to reduce wasted spend

-Launch new campaigns and test structures

-Improve click-through rate (CTR) and conversion rate (CVR)

--Provide weekly performance reports

-Suggest listing improvements that may increase conversions

-Monitor ACOS, TACOS, ROAS and profitability



Required Experience



-Minimum 1–2 years managing Amazon PPC campaigns

-Experience with US marketplace required

-Strong understanding of:

-Campaign structure

-Keyword match types

-Bid optimization

-Placement adjustments

-Ability to analyse data and make decisions independently

-Excellent written English

-Experience with Helium 10 and Junglescout 



Performance Expectations

-We care about results.

Your goal will be:

-Maintain profitable ACOS

-Reduce wasted ad spend

-Scale winning keywords

-Increase total sales through PPC

-Provide clear reporting and strategic recommendations



How to Apply



To be considered, please answer the following:



-What is your experience managing Amazon PPC?

-What ACOS range have you worked with?

-What would you do in the first 30 days if you were hired?

-Share one example of a campaign you improved and how you did it.

-What tools do you use?

-Include the word “FOOTPOWER” in your application so we know you read everything.



Applications without detailed answers will not be considered.

title: Executive Assistant to the Founder
description:
Forget What You Think an EA Does.



This isn't a role for someone who manages a calendar and waits for instructions. This is a role for someone who sees what needs to happen before they're asked, and makes it happen before anyone notices it was a problem.



We're growing fast. The Founder is deep in client delivery, sales, expansion, and content simultaneously. To keep scaling without losing quality, we need one person who owns the operational and administrative infrastructure around him.

That's you. If you're ready.



***About Growth Dinners***



Growth Dinners operates and runs curated executive dinner experiences for B2B companies across multiple U.S. cities. Our clients are sponsors and hosts who use our dinners to access hard-to-reach buyers, build relationships with C-suite prospects, and accelerate pipeline in a room where business actually gets done.



We're a lean, founder-led operation with high standards, fast decisions, and a bias toward action. Every hire matters here.

What You'll Own



***Event & Dinner Operations***



Coordinate logistics for B2B executive dinners end-to-end — venues, RSVPs, run-of-show, day-of execution, and post-event follow-up

Build and maintain event timelines, checklists, and SOPs that reduce friction and prevent anything from falling through the cracks

Manage contractor communication and vendor coordination before and after each event

Track event deliverables and ensure all client commitments are met on schedule



*** Founder Calendar & Inbox*** 



Own the Founder's calendar — proactively protect high-output time, schedule with intent, and eliminate scheduling back-and-forth

Triage and manage inbox communications — draft responses, flag priorities, and handle routine correspondence independently

Prepare the Founder for every meeting with relevant context, agenda, and background

Follow up on open loops so nothing stalls due to a missed reply

Client Communications & Follow-Up

Draft and manage client-facing communications with professionalism and attention to brand voice

Track client deliverables, deadlines, and milestones across active engagements

Coordinate onboarding touchpoints for new dinner sponsors and ensure a smooth client experience from contract to event day

Send post-event summaries, attribution documentation, and follow-up sequences on behalf of the Founder



*** CRM & Pipeline Management*** 



Keep the CRM clean, updated, and actionable — log activity, update deal stages, and flag stale contacts

Manage outreach sequences and ensure follow-ups are sent on schedule

Build and maintain contact lists for prospective dinner guests and event sponsors

Surface weekly pipeline reports and flag anything that needs the Founder's attention



** Who You Are** 



You're the kind of person who finishes something before being asked twice. You think in systems. You write with clarity. And you take ownership like it's your business — because in many ways, it will be.

You have 3–4 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Operations Coordinator, or similar role — ideally in a fast-moving startup, agency, or events environment

You're a strong writer who can draft
s, client communications, and follow-ups that sound like a human wrote them. 

Your AI is fluent and up to speed on how to use the latest AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc. to maximize efficiency and productivity. 

You understand basic automation tools like Zapier and Make.com or are excited to jump in and to learn them on your first day.

You have a working knowledge of CRM tools & SOPs databases (HubSpot, Notion, Airtable, or similar) and don't need to be trained from zero. 

You're proactive — you don't wait to be told the same thing twice

You communicate ahead of problems, not after

You've read Buy Back Your Time by Dan Martell or you're ready to — because that's the operating system we run on

You thrive in a remote environment and take ownership of your own structure and accountability



***What Success Looks Like in 30 Days***



The Founder's inbox and calendar are under control — no missed follow-ups, no double-bookings, no surprise deadlines

Every active dinner event has a documented run-of-show and client communication log

The CRM is clean, current, and being used to drive follow-up — not just store data

You've proactively identified and solved at least two recurring operational problems without being asked



***Our Hiring Process***

We move fast and we value your time. Here's exactly what to expect:

1.  Submit a 1-minute Loom video answering: Why are you the right fit for this role? What's a system or process you built that made someone's work life meaningfully easier?

2.  15-minute intro call — quick vibe check on expectations and fit

3.  Short skills assessment — we'll send you a realistic task (paid) to see how you think and execute

4.  Final interview with the Founder

5.  Offer — if we're both excited, we move immediately

Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

This role is remote, part-time 10 hours per week with ability to grow into full-time role. 

We work together on a paid project, then move to part-time, and finally full-time after you've demonstrated you're a fit. 

To apply, send your Loom video + resume to 
 with subject line: "EA Application — [Your Name]"

title: Senior Bookkeeper
description:
We are GOVA, an academy that trains and develops Filipino professionals to support U.S.-based businesses. We are not a recruitment agency. We are currently looking for an experienced Senior Bookkeeper / Accountant with U.S. tax experience to support U.S.-based accounting and tax operations.



Responsibilities



• Manage full-cycle bookkeeping for U.S. clients

• Perform bank and credit card reconciliations

• Prepare journal entries and adjusting entries

• Maintain and review the general ledger

• Prepare monthly financial statements

• Assist in preparing U.S. tax returns (1040, 1120, 1120S, 1065)

• Work closely with U.S.-based accountants and CPAs

• Prepare supporting documents for tax filings

• Assist with year-end closing and tax-ready financial reports

• Communicate with clients and internal teams regarding financial data

• Guide or support junior bookkeeping staff when necessary



Requirements



• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Accountancy, or related field

• 3–5 years experience with U.S. bookkeeping and tax preparation

• Experience working with a U.S. accountant, CPA firm, or tax preparation company

• Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP and U.S. tax regulations

• Experience using QuickBooks Desktop

• Familiarity with U.S. tax software such as Drake, Lacerte, ProSeries, UltraTax, or similar

• Excellent written and spoken English

• Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines



Preferred Qualifications



• Philippine CPA is a plus

• Experience handling multiple U.S. clients

• Experience supervising or guiding junior accounting staff

• Familiarity with sales tax filings



Position Details



• Full-time remote position (Philippines-based)

• Salary: USD $1,200 per month (Negotiable)

• 6 months probationary

• Must be available during U.S. business hours (Alaska time zone)

title: Non-voice Business Development Representative / Lead Generation Specialist (100% WFH)
description:
We’re looking for a motivated and results-driven BDR / Lead Generation Specialist to help grow our sales pipeline and expand our customer base. As a BDR, you’ll be on the front lines of our sales efforts, identifying potential clients, generating leads, and nurturing opportunities to connect them with our solutions.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Proactively research and identify potential customers in target markets.

2. Reach out to prospects via
s, LinkedIn, and other communication channels.

3. Qualify leads to assess fit and interest, ensuring alignment with our services.

4. Schedule meetings or product demos between qualified leads and Sales Development Representatives.

5. Maintain accurate records of lead interactions and update CRM systems accordingly.

6. Collaborate with the marketing and sales teams to execute campaigns and optimize lead generation strategies.

7. Stay up-to-date on industry trends and our product/service offerings to effectively communicate value propositions.



Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

- 2 - 3 years of experience in sales, business development, or a similar role (entry-level candidates with strong communication skills and a hunger to learn are also encouraged to apply).

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

- Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build rapport quickly.

- Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and eager to exceed targets.

- Proficiency in extracting data from LinkedIn Sales Navigator and other professional networks

- Must have a well-optimized LinkedIn account with atleast a minimum of 300 connections that can be used for prospecting

- Familiarity with CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, PipeDrive) is a plus.

- Comfortable with cold outreach and prospecting.



Who we are:

Acadium started with the belief that anyone, anywhere, should be able to get the skills and experience they need to start their career without barriers like price, schedule, or location getting in their way. Acadium gives anyone the tools, support, and mentorship they need to launch their dream career. Acadium is the only platform that provides real work experience, accessible to everyone. Acadium began in 2017 with a team of 3 co-founders: Richard Shaw, Marco Tundo, and Moe Abbas. Since then, they’ve attracted more than 120,000 student members and over 50,000 businesses to connect through the apprenticeship marketplace. Our team is growing and looking for people who are motivated, intelligent, and committed to making education accessible to join our team.



What we value:

Integrity - Be trustworthy in your actions

Care - Treat your teammates and our community as you would want to be treated

Curiosity - Ask questions. Always state your assumptions

Tenacity - Persevere when times are tough

Discipline - Be consistent in your actions

Ambition - Aspire to become better every day

Service -Serve your community to the best of your abilities.

title: Collections and Accounts Receivable Specialist
description:
About the Opportunity



Our amazing client is a nationally recognized leader in environmental and restoration services, specializing in property damage restoration, mold remediation, moisture control, and air quality improvement. With a strong commitment to helping property owners recover quickly and efficiently, they serve residential and commercial clients across the United States.



Their team is growing, and they are looking for dedicated professionals to support their mission of delivering exceptional service.





Role Overview



Our amazing client is seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Collections & Accounts Receivable Specialist to join their team remotely from the Philippines.



This role is critical to maintaining healthy cash flow by managing outstanding accounts, following up on insurance claims, and ensuring timely collections. A significant component of this role involves reviewing and interpreting insurance estimates, including understanding line items, pricing, scope of work, and coverage details.



The ideal candidate is a self-starter who thrives in a fast-paced environment, takes ownership of their work, and consistently delivers results.



Key Responsibilities



• Manage the full collections cycle, including outbound calls,
s, and follow-ups to recover outstanding balances from clients and insurance carriers.

• Review, interpret, and analyze insurance estimates and restoration project documentation, including line items, scope of work, pricing breakdowns, and coverage details.

• Cross-reference insurance estimates against invoices and job records to identify discrepancies, underpayments, or missing line items.

• Track, process, and follow up on property insurance claims, ensuring timely resolution and payment.

• Communicate with insurance adjusters, carriers, and third-party administrators to resolve claim disputes and negotiate payment.

• Maintain and update the CRM system daily with accurate notes, payment statuses, claim updates, and communication logs.

• Reconcile accounts receivable records and investigate discrepancies to ensure accurate billing.

• Coordinate with internal teams to resolve billing disputes, obtain supporting documentation, and expedite claim approvals.

• Generate accounts receivable aging reports and provide regular status updates to management.

• Identify delinquent accounts and implement appropriate escalation procedures.

• Maintain organized records of all collection activities and correspondence.

• Ensure compliance with company policies and best practices related to accounts receivable and collections.





Required Qualifications



• Minimum 2 years of experience in collections, accounts receivable, or a related financial/accounting operations role.

• Demonstrated experience reading, interpreting, and working with insurance estimates, particularly in the property restoration, construction, or insurance adjusting industry. This is a core function of the role and cannot be learned on the job.

• Experience working with property insurance claims, including communication with adjusters and carriers.

• Proficiency with CRM platforms and accounting software.

• Excellent verbal and written English communication skills.

• Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple accounts simultaneously.

• Self-motivated with a proven ability to meet deadlines and achieve targets with minimal supervision.

• Reliable high-speed internet connection and a dedicated, quiet home office setup.





Preferred Qualifications



• Prior experience as an insurance adjuster, claims processor, or estimator, or in a role that required daily review of Xactimate, Symbility, or similar estimating platforms.

• Experience supporting US-based companies in the property restoration, remediation, or home services industry.

• Familiarity with the full insurance claim lifecycle, from FNOL through settlement.

• Background in accounting operations, invoicing, or financial reporting.

• Experience working full-time US Eastern Time Zone hours.





What Our Client Offers



• Competitive monthly compensation based on experience and skill level.

• Full-time, stable position with a growing US-based company.

• Opportunity to make a meaningful impact on the business and grow within the organization.

• Supportive management with clear communication and expectations.

• A collaborative team environment that values accountability and results.





Work Requirements



• This is a full-time, dedicated position. The selected candidate must work exclusively for the company during business hours and may not hold concurrent employment.

• Time-tracking software will be installed from day one as part of standard company policy.

• Work hours align with US Eastern Time Zone (EST/EDT) business hours, approximately 40–45 hours per week.

title: Lead Generation Specialist / Business Development Representative
description:
We’re looking for a motivated and results-driven Lead Generation Specialist to help grow our sales pipeline and expand our customer base. As a BDR, you’ll be on the front lines of our sales efforts, identifying potential clients, generating leads, and nurturing opportunities to connect them with our solutions.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Proactively research and identify potential customers in target markets.

2. Reach out to prospects via
s, LinkedIn, and other communication channels.

3. Qualify leads to assess fit and interest, ensuring alignment with our services.

4. Schedule meetings or product demos between qualified leads and Sales Development Representatives.

5. Maintain accurate records of lead interactions and update CRM systems accordingly.

6. Collaborate with the marketing and sales teams to execute campaigns and optimize lead generation strategies.

7. Stay up-to-date on industry trends and our product/service offerings to effectively communicate value propositions.



Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

- 2 - 3 years of experience in sales, business development, or a similar role (entry-level candidates with strong communication skills and a hunger to learn are also encouraged to apply).

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

- Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build rapport quickly.

- Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and eager to exceed targets.

- Proficiency in extracting data from LinkedIn Sales Navigator and other professional networks

- Must have a well-optimized LinkedIn account with atleast a minimum of 300 connections that can be used for prospecting

- Familiarity with CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, PipeDrive) is a plus.

- Comfortable with cold outreach and prospecting.



Who we are:

Acadium started with the belief that anyone, anywhere, should be able to get the skills and experience they need to start their career without barriers like price, schedule, or location getting in their way. Acadium gives anyone the tools, support, and mentorship they need to launch their dream career. Acadium is the only platform that provides real work experience, accessible to everyone. Acadium began in 2017 with a team of 3 co-founders: Richard Shaw, Marco Tundo, and Moe Abbas. Since then, they’ve attracted more than 120,000 student members and over 50,000 businesses to connect through the apprenticeship marketplace. Our team is growing and looking for people who are motivated, intelligent, and committed to making education accessible to join our team.



What we value:

Integrity - Be trustworthy in your actions

Care - Treat your teammates and our community as you would want to be treated

Curiosity - Ask questions. Always state your assumptions

Tenacity - Persevere when times are tough

Discipline - Be consistent in your actions

Ambition - Aspire to become better every day

Service -Serve your community to the best of your abilities.



Interested applicants may send updated CVs and LinkedIn URLs with a minimum of 300 connections to 

 Subject: [Adjective that describes you] B2B Lead Generation Specialist

**Note: Only applicants who submit their resumes and LinkedIn URL will be evaluated.

title: Shopify Website Builder / Figma Website Designer
description:
Shopify Website Builder / Figma Website Designer



Remote – Freelance / Part-Time



About Us



We are a fast-growing international e-commerce group building multiple brands in sectors such as travel, fitness, electronics and lifestyle.

Our brands focus on premium consumer products including luggage, fitness equipment, home products and accessories. We are continuously launching new brands and improving existing e-commerce stores.

To support our growth, we are looking for a highly skilled Shopify Website Builder / Figma Designer who can help us design and build high-converting websites.

Your Role

You will help us design and build modern, premium Shopify stores that focus on conversion, branding and user experience.

You should understand how to combine design, UX and e-commerce strategy.

Responsibilities



Website Design (Figma)



• Design modern and conversion-focused websites in Figma

• Create full website structures including:

o Homepage

o Product pages

o Category pages

o Landing pages

o Blog layouts

• Design mobile-first layouts

• Create UI components such as:

o Buttons

o Icons

o Sections

o Product highlights

o Conversion elements



Shopify Development

• Build and launch websites using Shopify

• Translate Figma designs into Shopify themes

• Improve website performance and loading speed

• Configure Shopify apps

• Optimize existing Shopify stores

Conversion Optimization

• Improve product page conversions

• Optimize customer journey

• Implement upsells and bundles

• Improve checkout experience

Requirements

We are looking for someone who:

• Has strong Shopify experience

• Has strong Figma design skills

• Understands e-commerce conversion optimization

• Can design modern premium looking stores

• Understands mobile-first UX

• Communicates clearly and works efficiently

Bonus skills

• Shopify Liquid coding

• SEO knowledge

• CRO experience

• Landing page optimization



Workload

We are looking for someone available for:

20 hours per month

or

40 hours per month

What We Offer

• Long-term collaboration

• Multiple brand projects

• Flexible remote work

• Opportunity to work on fast-growing international brands

Application Requirements

To apply, please send:

1. Your portfolio

2. Links to Shopify stores you built

3. Examples of Figma designs

4. Screenshots or links of e-commerce websites you designed

5. Your monthly rate for 20 hours

6. Your monthly rate for 40 hours

Applications without examples of previous work will not be considered.



Screening Questions

Please answer the following:

1. Which Shopify websites have you built? (links)

2. Can you share Figma projects you designed?

3. How many Shopify stores have you worked on?

4. What is your monthly rate for 20 hours and 40 hours?

5. When can you start working with us?

6. Willing to move to DUBAI ?



Redjumla Wholesale FZCO – UAE DUBAI

title: Operations Quality Checker —  $1,000/month | Daily work | Long-term role
description:
What this job is

We run a remote team that operates across multiple platforms every day — posting content, handling client conversations, and managing follow-ups. Your job is to check that everything was done correctly.

Every morning, you review the previous day's activity against a checklist and send us a report flagging anything that was missed, late, or done wrong. That's the whole job. It's the same process every day.

This is not a creative role. It's a precision role. We're looking for someone who is genuinely good at spotting things that are slightly off, works to a strict daily deadline without being reminded, and takes pride in a clean, thorough report.



What you'll be checking every day



That all scheduled posts were published at the right time

That client conversations had no delays, no script errors, and no missed handoffs

That follow-up messages were sent at the right time with the right content

That the support account had no unanswered messages beyond the allowed threshold





Who we're looking for

You have at least 1 year of experience in a QA, operations checking, or compliance monitoring role — ideally in a BPO, VA, or remote operations context. You've been responsible for checking other people's work on a daily or recurring basis, and you know what it feels like to be accountable for missing something.

You are methodical by nature. You have a system. You don't skip steps when you're in a hurry.

You have a stable work setup: a proper computer, reliable internet, and the ability to have multiple tabs and apps open simultaneously.



Practical requirements



Available every day is great, 6 days minimum

Can complete the daily check and submit the report before 9:00 AM your time

Comfortable working across multiple platforms simultaneously (messaging apps, spreadsheets, browser tabs)

Strong English reading ability

Based in a timezone that allows morning availability (Philippines strongly preferred)



Before you apply — record your Loom first

Before filling out the application form, record a short Loom video (2 to 3 minutes). You will be asked to paste the link in the form. Applications without a Loom will not be reviewed.

Loom is free at 
. If you have never used it, it takes less than 5 minutes to set up.

Your video must cover the following four points:

-Who you are and where you are based

-What role you held where you were responsible for checking someone else's work — be specific about what you were checking and how often

-What your daily process looked like in that role — how you organized yourself, what tools you used, how you reported findings

-Why you are interested in this position



Keep it under 3 minutes. Speak clearly. You do not need to be on camera if you prefer screen recording, but your voice must be audible.



Pay and structure

$1,000/month, paid biweekly. Starting with a paid 3-day trial at an hourly rate. Bonus system on top of base pay for specific types of findings — details shared during onboarding.

This is a long-term role. We're not looking for someone to try it for a month. If you do the job well, you stay.



To apply

Record your Loom first, then fill in the application form where we will ask for some more information and ask you to submit your loom link. Here is the form: 



We review in batches. If your profile fits, you'll hear from us within 24-48H

Only apply if you have direct, verifiable experience checking or auditing someone else's work in a remote role.

title: Social Media Engagement Specialist
description:
Remote. 10 hours per week. US working hours required. Growth opportunity available.



We are hiring a Social Media Engagement Specialist to help grow and nurture our personal brand and women’s wellness brand, LadyBoss.



This is not a content posting role.



This role is about conversation, connection, and community.



Your job is to:

• Engage with new followers daily

• Respond to comments and DMs in brand voice

• Leave thoughtful comments on aligned accounts

• Start real conversations, not generic replies

• Invite qualified women into our LadyBoss 
munity

• Track daily engagement metrics

• Documenting outreach SOPs



You will be the voice behind two growing brands. You must write in fluent, natural English. Robotic or scripted communication will not be accepted.



What We Are Looking For:

• Excellent written English with strong grammar

• High emotional intelligence

• Ability to adjust tone depending on brand

• Comfortable working US hours

• Organized and self directed

• Open to feedback and performance metrics



This role starts at 10 hours per week with opportunity to expand.



Application Process:



Step 1: Submit a 2 to 3 minute Loom video answering:

 1. How do you use Instagram daily?

 2. How would you start conversations in comments that feel natural?

 3. How would you guide someone from casual engagement to a next step without sounding sales focused?

 4. How do you adjust your voice when representing someone else’s brand?



Step 2: Written responses after Loom review.

title: Shopify Web Developer - CRO Specialist - Ecommerce Advertising Agency - Full Time 1a
description:
APPLY ON MY WEBSITE TO BE CONSIDERED: 


/cro-shopify-web-developer/



CRO Shopify Web Developer – Job Overview

I am looking for an experienced shopify web developer to help me upgrade client’s checkout processes and execute CRO tests for various ecommerce brands.

In this role you will update Shopify theme templates, develop landing pages using various page builders and html/CSS frameworks, research/install various checkout/bundle applications, and implement A/B tests using testing tools like intelligems.

You will also report on test performance, and provide suggestions for next steps.

This is a full-time opportunity. Training will be provided and you will work directly with me.



About Zack Miller

My name is Zack Miller, and I’ve been a DTC Ecommerce Growth Marketer since 2019. I’ve done everything fro
dia buying and client strategy, to agency internal operations.

I’ve contributed to the growth of over 200 ecommerce brands in my agency experience. Now, as an independent growth marketing partner, I work closely with a small number of brands on their new customer acquisition strategy.



Note: All Remote Employees Must Be Available To Work During The Following Business Hours:

Monday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Tuesday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Wednesday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Thursday: 9am – 5:30pm EST

Friday: 9am – 5:30pm EST



Roles & Responsibilities

- Develop landing pages using Figma and various WYSYWIG editors including but not limited to Replo and Pagefly.

- Edit product, collection and other page templates using Shopify Liquid template engine, and various HTML/CSS frameworks.

- Install various checkout/tracking applications (aftersell, Zipify OCU, Upstack, etc)

- Install various applications for product bundling when needed, and create product bundles. 

- Install tracking pixels on client’s websites

- Implement A/B tests in intelligems for product price tests, shipping price tests, theme tests, landing page tests, and more. 

- Research new ideas for direct response content marketing strategies.

- Work in my project management system, ClickUp.

- Apply a process-first mentality and provide feedback to help further develop our operating systems.



Required Experience & Education

- Strong verbal and written English language skills (American Dialect)

- Strong knowledge of HTML/CSS/Javascript/Shopify Liquid required. 

- Knowledge of Python and Ruby preferred but not required.

- 1+ years of web development (Shopify) experience required

- 1+ years of backend python (flask/django) development experience preferred but not required

- 1+ years of marketing agency experience preferred but not required

- Must be highly organized and possess strong attention to detail

- All applicants are encouraged to apply, regardless of experience or education



APPLY ON MY WEBSITE TO BE CONSIDERED: 


/cro-shopify-web-developer/a

title: Amazon PPC Specialist
description:
Dear Applicants,



## Amazon PPC Specialist WANTED!! ##



**Candidates with experience OR taken a PPC course before will be prioritised.



Training & orientation to our SOPs are also provided - but you must have at least basic PPC common sense and basic knowledge (like what's a click, what's CTR, what's the difference between negative exact/negative phrase matches etc) to begin with.



We are an e-commerce company that is selling primarily on Amazon FBA (private label) that's looking for a full-time tea
mber as a PPC Specialist to join us to build the company together.



We value people who are goal-oriented and can work well with minimal supervision.



JOB SCOPE:



- Executing a product launch SOP through Amazon Ads

- Driving traffic efficiently to product listings

- Monitor, Analyze and implement Strategic campaigns to make our products visible and increase sales.

- Set a roadmap and execute a plan of action to run and improve campaigns over a period of time.



Note:

Full-time basis - 40 hrs/week (Mon-Fri)

Working hours: 10 am to 6:30 pm (30 min. lunch break)

14 days of Paid Annual Leave and Paid Public Holidays Benefits (all major holidays in the Philippines) included



**IMPORTANT**: When applying, please provide:

1. Your CV/Resume

2. A short video introduction of yourself and why you think you're a good fit.

3. Did you take any Amazon PPC course before? If so, which one?

4. Elaborate about your relevant past experience (if any) and why you'd be the best fit for this position. If you do not have experience, please be honest and state so as well.



**Applications without the above 4 points will be less considered.



P.S. Please format your replies in point form according to the 4 items asked above, e.g:



1. My resume is here:

2. Video introduction here:

3. I am doing... etc

4. I have... etc



Best regards,

Kevan

title: AI Content Creator
description:
Dear Applicants,



## AI Content Creator – We're Hiring! ##



We are an e-commerce company looking for a creative and driven AI Content Creator to join our full-time team. You'll be at the forefront of our content production, using the latest AI tools to create compelling videos and visuals that captivate audiences and drive results.



IMPORTANT: If you lack experience, you at least need to have a strong creative instinct and the ability to learn quickly. We value responsible, self-motivated people who take ownership of their work and can deliver quality content with minimal supervision.



Job Scope:



- Create high-quality AI-generated visuals and video content using tools such as Higgsfield, Midjourney, Canva, CapCut, Caption AI, and ElevenLabs

- Edit and produce short, engaging videos that are polished and ready to publish across social media platforms

- Generate and refine AI voiceovers to complement video content

- Manage content from concept to completion — ideating, producing, and delivering ready-to-post content end-to-end

- Stay up to date with the latest AI content tools and trends, and proactively suggest new ways to improve content quality and performance

- Write clear, compelling, and engaging copy for captions, scripts, hooks, and product content.



Note:



- Full-time basis – 40 hrs/week

- Availability required across Monday to Sunday (schedule to be discussed)

- 14 days of Paid Annual Leave and Paid Public Holidays Benefits (all major holidays in the Philippines) included



IMPORTANT: When applying, please provide:



Your CV/Resume

- A short video introduction of yourself and why you think you're a good fit

- Why you are leaving your last job or what you were doing just before this application

- A portfolio or examples of AI-generated content you have created previously. If you do not have prior experience, please be honest and state so — we are open to fast learners with the right attitude



Applications without the above points will be less considered.

P.S. Please format your replies in point form according to the items asked above, e.g.:



My resume is here:

Video introduction here:

I am currently doing... / I am leaving because...

Here are examples of my work...



The more information you provide in your initial application, the better.

Best regards,

Loo Yi

title: Virtual Assistant (Content, Canva & Social Media Support)
description:

This role is perfect for someone who enjoys a mix of content creation, Canva design, social media support, and organization. You’ll work closely with the founder as a right hand and support both our content and our student community.



What you’ll help with

Team Speak Fluent German

title: Ecommerce Operations Manager
description:
Grassias is a fast-growing UK ecommerce brand selling across Amazon, eBay, and TikTok Shop. We are a lean, high-revenue team and we are looking for a sharp, reliable Operations Manager to take full ownership of our day-to-day operations so our founders can focus on growth.



This is not a passive VA role. You will be the operational backbone of the business. We need someone who takes initiative, spots problems before they happen, and keeps the machine running without being chased.



Responsibilities



Customer Service

- Answer and resolve all customer messages across eBay, Amazon, and

- Maintain platform seller metrics and response rates



Platform Management

- Day-to-day management of Amazon, eBay, and TikTok Shop

- Ensure listings are live, accurate, and performing



Returns & Refunds

- Track and manage all returns and refunds across all platforms

- Maintain 100% visibility on return status at all times



Stock Management

-Daily stock checks - top products must never go out of stock

- Verify all listings remain active and in stock

- Monthly warehouse stock report confirming quantities and value



Review & Competitor Management

- Weekly review check across all platforms with compiled report

- Weekly competitor analysis - flag changes and make recommendations



Inbox Management

- Maintain inbox zero across two inboxes (customer services + founder)

- Delete, unsubscribe, and organise

- Create tasks from important
s

- Send daily summary to founder



Shipment & Logistics

- Book Amazon FBA shipments

- Track stock movement and keep records updated

- Liaise with haulage companies for updates



Daily Reporting

- Send a daily report covering sales and open tasks

- Flag anything requiring founder attention immediately



Requirements

- Proven Amazon Seller Central experience - FBA essential

- Excellent written English

- Strong SOP creation skills

- Proactive, detail-oriented, self-managed

- AI tools experience



Bonus

- eBay and TikTok Shop experience

- Claude code, Lovable, or n8n experience

- Bookkeeping or Hubdoc experience



Role:

Full Time (37.5 hours)

UK Hours (8am - 4pm)

Salary $900 - $1200 per month (depending on experience)



How to Apply

Reply to job post with Ops Manager Application — [Your Name]



Include:

Short intro video introduction yourself on your ecommerce ops experience

Availability and UK overlap hours you can cover

Tools and automations you have used

Expected monthly rate



Applications without Amazon FBA experience or Intro video will not be considered.

title: AI Content & Marketing Assistant (Full-Time Remote)
description:
We are building **AI-powered marketing systems** and need a **reliable Content & Marketing Specialist** to help run and grow them.



Your main focus will be **social media, content workflows, and light video editing**, while also supporting the systems behind the scenes. You’ll help turn long-form content into **short videos, social media posts, blogs, and
s**, while working alongside our team to build smarter marketing processes using AI tools.



You don’t need to know every tool already. What matters most is **strong English, reliability, a good work ethic, and a willingness to learn and evolve with AI tools.**



You’ll be working with a **small remote team, including other Filipino tea
mbers**, supporting the same systems and projects.



---



## What You'll Be Doing



• Editing **YouTube videos into short-form content** for social media

• Light video editing using **CapCut or your preferred editing tool**

• Scheduling and managing **social media content**

• Helping build systems that turn long-form content into **blogs,
s, and social posts**

• Supporting **funnels, landing pages, and websites**

• Assisting with marketing systems inside **GoHighLevel**

• Uploading and organising **Meta /
 ads**

• Using tools such as **ChatGPT, Claude, N8N and other AI tools**

• Creating organised workflows for content and marketing tasks

• Providing **light customer support when needed**



Tools commonly used include:

• **CapCut (or your preferred editing tool)**

• **Canva**

• **ChatGPT / Claude**

• **N8N**

• **GoHighLevel**



Experience with **GoHighLevel is a bonus**, but **not required**.

Experience with **social media, content editing, marketing systems, or AI tools** is more important.



---



## Working Pattern



• **Full-time role — 8 hours per day**

• **Core working hours: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM UK time**

• Some **flexibility around these hours is possible**

• **$6–$8 per hour** depending on experience

• Remote role



This role must be your **primary full-time focus**.

We are **not looking for someone managing multiple full-time clients.**



---



## Availability Expectations



Most work happens Monday–Friday, but there are **occasional small responsibilities outside normal hours**.



For example:



• We run a **webinar every Sunday**, which requires around **45–60 minutes of support**

• Occasionally small things may need checking outside working hours



This does **not mean working full weekends**, but we need someone **reliable and flexible when needed.**



---



## How To Apply (Read Carefully)



To confirm you’ve read this properly:



1. Apply through OnlineJobs.ph



2. Use this subject line:



**“I Am Your Content Specialist”**



3. Record a **Loom video answering the following questions (spoken on video):**



• Tell me why you think you are a good fit for this role

• Tell me something you are passionate about outside work

• Tell me something you have stayed disciplined with for a long period of time

• What experience do you have with **social media, video editing, marketing, GoHighLevel, or AI tools?**

• Why do you believe you would be a good fit for this role?



4. Make sure your **DISC assessment is completed on your OnlineJobs.ph profile** before applying.



Applications that **do not include the subject line, Loom video answers, or DISC profile will not be considered.**

title: Guest Communication & Hotel Operations Coordinator – Virtual Assistant
description:
ABOUT THE WANDERER

The Wanderer is a boutique hotel in Kennebunk, Maine, known for its timeless charm, relaxed coastal feel, and elevated guest experience. This role will join the management team responsible for helping deliver that experience each day while supporting the systems and operations that make it possible.

The Wanderer is more than a hotel. It is the first property in a broader vision to build a world-class, tech-enabled, remotely managed boutique hospitality platform powered by smart systems and AI. Our goal is to create a guest experience that feels thoughtful, elevated, and highly personal while keeping the on-site operational footprint intentionally lean.

We are building with the future in mind. That means moving quickly, thinking creatively, refining constantly, and embracing innovation across both the guest experience and hotel operations. This first hotel is the foundation for what we hope will become a growing portfolio of exceptional boutique properties.

We are looking for tea
mbers who are energized by that pace and excited by the opportunity to help build something modern, ambitious, and operationally excellent from the ground up.



POSITION SUMMARY

The Guest Communication & Hotel Operations Coordinator will serve as the voice of The Wanderer and play a key role in shaping the guest experience through every interaction. This person will be responsible not only for responding to guests, but for representing the brand with consistency, polish, warmth, and sound judgment.



Every communication should reflect The Wanderer’s values, tone, personality, and overall guest experience standards so that guests feel a clear and consistent sense of the brand from inquiry through post-stay follow-up.

This role will be primarily focused on guest communication and guest experience support, which is expected to represent approximately 75% of the role initially. The remaining 25% will support hotel operations coordination, reporting, vendor communication, and process development. As the business evolves, the scope may expand based on business needs and the candidate’s strengths.



The ideal candidate is warm, polished, detail-oriented, highly organized, and solution-focused. Because this role is highly guest-facing, exceptional written and spoken English communication skills and the ability to embody The Wanderer’s brand voice are essential.



CORE PURPOSE

To delight, inform, and support guests throughout their journey while helping create a highly organized, efficient, and well-executed operating environment for the property.



KEY RESPONSIBILTIES

This role is split into two primary buckets: guest communication and guest experience (primary focus, approximately 75%), and hotel operations coordination (secondary support function, approximately 25%).



1. GUEST COMMUNICATION & GUEST EXPERIENCE



Brand Representation & Communication Standards

• Serve as a true extension of The Wanderer brand in all guest-facing communication.

• Ensure every guest interaction reflects the brand’s values, tone, personality, and service standards.

• Communicate in a way that creates a consistent, elevated, and memorable guest experience across all channels.

• Balance warmth, professionalism, hospitality, and clarity in every interaction.

• Use strong judgment to ensure responses are not only accurate and helpful, but also aligned with the type of experience The Wanderer wants guests to have.



Guest Communication

• Serve as the primary point of contact for current and prospective guests via phone,
, SMS, and booking platforms.

• Respond to inquiries about the hotel experience, including pricing, policies, accommodations, amenities, location, and differentiators.

• Provide clear, friendly, and accurate information that helps guests feel confident in booking and excited about their stay.

• Offer thoughtful recommendations for local restaurants, activities, and area attractions.

• Maintain fast response times and ensure all guest communication is handled promptly, thoroughly, and professionally.



Property & Experience Expertise

• Develop deep knowledge of the entire hotel experience, including room types, amenities, parking, common spaces, property features, supplies, and guest how-to information.

• Become highly knowledgeable in all guest-facing policies and procedures so questions can be answered confidently and consistently.

• Understand common guest issues, how to troubleshoot them, and when to escalate concerns.

• Identify opportunities to enhance the guest experience, such as noting celebrations, special requests, or personalized touches.



Booking & Reservation Management

• Manage booking-related communication, including new reservations, modifications, cancellations, payment-related questions, and calendar coordination.

• Become highly proficient in the Property Management System and related tools, with the ability to create and modify reservations, cancel bookings, manage calendar availability, review guest notes and booking details, and run basic reports as needed.

• Ensure accuracy across reservations, guest details, and internal notes.



Review & Reputation Management

• Respond to guest reviews in a timely, professional, and brand-aligned manner.

• Monitor guest feedback trends and flag recurring issues or opportunities for improvement.

• Help implement solutions based on feedback to continuously strengthen the guest experience.

• Assist with guest recovery efforts when issues arise, with the goal of resolving concerns thoughtfully and protecting the brand’s reputation.



Guest Issue Coordination

• Act as the communication hub for guest-related questions, requests, and issues.

• Receive, assess, and route guest needs to the appropriate tea
mber or vendor, including matters related to maintenance, inventory, housekeeping, or other property concerns.

• Ensure important information is communicated clearly and efficiently so issues are resolved in a timely manner.

• Track guest-related issues through completion and help ensure strong follow-through.



2. HOTEL OPERATIONS COORDINATION



Operations Coordination & Vendor Management

• Help facilitate efficient hotel operations through strong communication, organization, and follow-through.

• Coordinate with internal tea
mbers, cleaners, maintenance personnel, and third-party vendors to ensure tasks, projects, and issues are handled promptly and correctly.

• Communicate daily and weekly operational needs clearly, including cleanings, maintenance items, supply needs, and property-related updates.

• Support adherence to operational processes, including software usage, on-time task completion, checklist submission, photo uploads, and system updates.

• Monitor inventory and operational needs proactively and communicate gaps or issues to the appropriate tea
mbers.

• Help ensure the property is consistently set up for success behind the scenes and that operational issues are tracked through resolution.



Systems, Reporting & Process Improvement

• Assist with OTA and PMS support, helping ensure listings, reservations, and core systems are functioning properly.

• Monitor OTA listings and flag opportunities for improvement in visibility, accuracy, and guest conversion.

• Help process guest claims and support resolution case management as needed.

• Monitor operational items such as lock and camera battery levels and ensure issues are communicated and resolved.

• Assist with KPI reporting, weekly meeting preparation, and basic operational analysis.

• Support price optimization and competitor analysis.

• Help operationalize the business through documentation and process improvement, including the creation and ongoing maintenance of SOPs, written instructions, and video tutorials.

• Provide general administrative support and ad hoc operational analysis as needed.



REQUIREMENTS & QUALIFICATIONS

• Prior experience in guest support within hotels, Airbnb, or short-term rental management is required.

• Experience working with hospitality and short-term rental platforms such as Airbnb, 
, Expedia, and VRBO.

• Professional-level mastery of written and spoken English is required, with the ability to communicate clearly, professionally, and in a brand-aligned manner.

• Outstanding customer service skills and a hospitality-first mindset.

• Extreme attention to detail and strong organizational skills.

• Excellent typing speed and accuracy.

• Strong problem-solving skills and sound judgment.

• Ability to read critically, interpret information carefully, and respond thoughtfully.

• Comfortable working across multiple systems and technology platforms.

• Able to manage multiple conversations, tasks, and priorities at once while maintaining accuracy and professionalism.



SYSTEMS & PLATFORM EXPERIENCE

Candidates should have prior experience working with hospitality and short-term rental systems, along with the ability to learn new platforms quickly. While direct experience with Newbook is a plus, it is not required. More importantly, we are looking for someone with a working knowledge of Property Management Systems and the core concepts behind them, so they can navigate tools confidently and adapt quickly with training.

Preferred experience includes:

• OTA platforms such as Airbnb, 
, Expedia, and VRBO

• Property Management Systems such as Newbook (our PMS), Hospitable, Cloudbeds, or similar tools

• Communication tools such as
, phone systems, and WhatsApp

• Operational and support tools such as 
, PriceLabs, or similar software

• Google Workspace, including Google Drive, Google Docs, and Google Sheets



IDEAL CANDIDATE TRAITS

• Able to represent The Wanderer brand with consistency, professionalism, good judgment, and strong attention to tone.

• Warm, polished, and hospitality-driven demeanor.

• Highly detail-oriented, organized, and dependable.

• Comfortable handling multiple conversations and tasks at once.

• Confident problem-solver who remains calm and resourceful under pressure.

• Able to learn systems quickly and follow processes consistently.

• Proactive, resourceful, and comfortable coordinating across multiple moving parts.

• Excited by fast-moving, entrepreneurial environments where systems, processes, and ideas are evolving in real time.

• Motivated by the opportunity to help build something innovative, modern, and scalable.



SCHEDULE / SCOPE

• This role is expected to be primarily guest-facing at the outset, with approximately 80% of responsibilities focused on guest communication and 20% focused on hotel operations support.

• Scope may expand over time based on business needs and the candidate’s strengths.

• Specific schedule, hours, and coverage expectations will be discussed during the hiring process.

title: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
description:
Chief Financial Officer (CFO) ????

Industry: Real Estate Development / Warehouse & Distribution / Commercial Leasing

Reports to: CEO

Location: Remote (Philippines) ???????? — Must work U.S. hours



???? Company Overview

We are a growing real estate development and investment company focused on warehouse distribution facilities, commercial leasing, and ground-up development projects.

Our projects involve land acquisition, construction and development, tenant leasing, and long-term asset management.

We are seeking an experienced Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to lead financial strategy, capital raising, project financing, and investor fund creation to support our expansion.



???? Position Summary

The CFO will oversee all financial operations including project financing, investor fund structuring, bank lending relationships, construction finance, and real estate financial management.

This role requires strong experience in warehouse or commercial real estate development and capital markets.

The CFO will work closely with the CEO to secure financing, structure investment vehicles, and drive financial growth across multiple development projects.

Strategic Financial Leadership

Education

???? Key Skills

• Capital raising and fund structuring

???? Preferred Deal Experience

Experience with:

Applications without a recording will not be considered.

title: CRO / Website Specialist (GoHighLevel + WordPress)
description:
We’re a growing U.S.-based marketing agency that helps service-based businesses grow through paid ads, landing pages, websites, funnels, and systems.



We’re looking for a CRO / Website Specialist who is very strong in GoHighLevel and WordPress.



Your main role will be building, editing, improving, and QA-ing websites and landing pages with a strong focus on conversion.



This is not mainly a graphic design role.

This is not mainly a Google Ads management role.



We want someone who is strongest at:

 • building pages in GHL

 • editing and improving sites in WordPress

 • making pages look clean and professional

 • improving mobile responsiveness

 • thinking through layout, flow, CTA placement, and conversion



You do not need to be an advanced Google Ads strategist, but it is a strong bonus if you understand the basics of Google Ads and how landing pages support paid traffic.



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants to grow with a fast-moving agency and play an important role in helping client campaigns perform better.



(Directions on how to apply are at the bottom of this posting.)



JOB REQUIREMENTS

 • Strong hands-on experience with GoHighLevel

 • Strong hands-on experience with WordPress

 • Experience building and editing websites, landing pages, and funnels

 • Strong eye for layout, spacing, section structure, and mobile optimization

 • Able to build clean, high-converting pages from examples, briefs, or direction

 • Experience with forms, buttons, embeds, calendars, and basic integrations

 • Strong attention to detail and ability to QA your own work

 • Fast and accurate with revisions

 • Comfortable working across multiple client projects

 • Fluent written and spoken English

 • Good communicator

 • Reliable internet and dependable work habits

 • Available full time Monday–Friday



BONUS SKILLS (Nice to Have)

 • Basic to intermediate Google Ads experience

 • Understanding of how landing pages connect to paid traffic

 • Familiarity with conversion tracking

 • Experience with GA4, GTM, pixels, or event setup

 • Understanding of CRO / conversion principles

 • Experience with service business lead generation

 • Ability to spot weak headlines, poor CTA placement, cluttered layouts, or friction points on a page



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 • Building and editing websites in GoHighLevel and WordPress

 • Creating landing pages for paid traffic campaigns

 • Improving existing pages for better conversion

 • Making sites mobile responsive and clean across devices

 • Setting up forms, calendars, buttons, and page sections

 • Supporting website launches and page updates

 • QA-ing pages before they go live

 • Making ongoing edits and improvements for clients

 • Supporting ad-to-page funnel execution

 • Occasionally assisting with Google Ads-related tasks if you have experience there



IMPORTANT



This role is best for someone who is:

 • very strong in GHL

 • very solid in WordPress

 • interested in conversion-focused page building

 • at least somewhat familiar with Google Ads



We are looking for someone who can help us execute quickly, improve page quality, and support better campaign performance.



HOW TO APPLY

 1.
 us at 

 2. Use this subject line: CRO / Website Specialist – GHL + WordPress

 3. Attach your resume

 4. Include your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

 5. Include links to 3–5 pages or websites you have built or edited in GoHighLevel or WordPress

 6. Include a screenshot of your internet speed. 

 7.  In your
, answer these questions:

- How many years of experience do you have with GoHighLevel?

- How many years of experience do you have with WordPress?

- Which platform are you stronger in: GHL or WordPress?

- Have you built landing pages for paid ads before?

- What do you usually look for when trying to improve a landing page’s conversion rate?

- How much Google Ads experience do you have? (Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced)

 8. Send us a short message here on OnlineJobs.ph letting us know you applied by



We’ll review applications and reply by
 with next steps for qualified candidates.



Thank you and we look forward to hearing from you.

title: Real Estate Transaction Coordinator
description:
Real Estate Transaction Coordinator (Land Investing)



We are looking for a highly organized Transaction Coordinator to help manage and move real estate deals from contract to closing. Are you able to think outside the box? 



Our company specializes in buying and selling land across the United States, and this role is critical to keeping deals organized, on schedule, and moving forward.



You will work directly with the founder and team to coordinate transactions, communicate with contractors and title companies, and manage project timelines.



This is a remote role with long-term growth potential for the right person.



Responsibilities

Transaction Coordination



Manage land transactions from contract to closing



Communicate with title companies and real estate agents



Track deadlines and ensure documents are completed on time



Monitor deal progress and follow up on outstanding items



Project Coordination



Call contractors for quotes, updates, and scheduling



Help coordinate property improvements (clearing, surveys, signage, etc.)



Track project timelines and ensure work is completed



Property Marketing Support



Upload and update listing photos



Light editing of photos when needed



Assist with preparing listings for sale



Deal Support



Assist with overseeing property purchases



Organize documents and maintain deal records



Keep internal spreadsheets and systems updated



Qualifications



Highly organized and detail-oriented



Strong communication skills (phone,
, and text)



Comfortable managing multiple deals and deadlines



Able to follow up and keep projects moving forward



Self-motivated and reliable



Required Communication Skills



This role requires frequent phone communication with U.S.-based businesses, including:



Title companies



Real estate agents



Contractors and service providers



Applicants must be comfortable speaking on the phone in English with U.S. companies and able to communicate clearly and professionally.



DO NOT DIRECT MESSAGE--> Please
:  



Preferred Experience



Real estate transaction coordination



Experience working with title companies



Familiarity with land deals or vacant land



Basic photo editing skills



Experience using spreadsheets or CRM systems

title: Video & Content Editor (AI Software Experience Required)
description:
Location: Remote



About the Role:



Our company is on the lookout for a creative, detail-driven Video & Content Editor who’s not only skilled in traditional editing tools but also experienced with today’s most innovative AI-driven platforms. If you’re a visual storyteller who thrives in fast-paced environments and knows your way around both classic software and cutting-edge AI tools, we want you on our team.



Responsibilities:



-Edit and produce high-quality video content for social media, website, and marketing campaigns using CapCut and Photoshop

-Create compelling digital designs, graphics, and layouts with Canva

-Utilize AI tools like ChatGPT Image Generation, Kling AI, Runway AI, and others for content creation and enhancement

-Collaborate closely with our marketing and creative teams to develop visually engaging content aligned with our brand standards

-Stay ahead of digital trends, AI advancements, and content innovation opportunities

-Manage multiple projects while maintaining deadlines and quality standards



Requirements:



-Proven experience as a video editor, graphic designer, or content creator

-Strong eye for detail, design aesthetics, and storytelling

-Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced, remote environment

-Excellent communication and collaboration skills



Preferred:

-Familiarity with social media content strategy and digital marketing



Why Work with Us?



We’re a bold, fast-growing brand passionate about style, storytelling, and delivering top-tier experiences to our audience. Join a creative, collaborative remote team where your work makes an immediate impact, and your ideas help shape our visual identity.

title: HR & Training Coordinator
description:
An HR and Training Coordinator for a hospice manages the full employee lifecycle, focusing on recruiting, onboarding, and training staff in compliance with healthcare regulations. They ensure high-quality patient care by coordinating mandatory training, maintaining personnel records, managing benefits, and fostering employee engagement in a compassionate care environment. 



Key Responsibilities

Recruitment & Staffing: Source, interview, and onboard new hospice employees (nurses, aides, social workers) to meet staffing needs and compliance standards.

Training & Education: Develop, coordinate, and deliver training programs, including orientation for new hires, in-services on hospice care, safety protocols, and regulatory updates (CMS, state laws).

Regulatory Compliance: Maintain employee personnel files in accordance with hospice regulations and accreditation standards, ensuring certifications, licenses, and background checks are up-to-date.

Employee Relations & Engagement: Serve as the primary contact for HR-related questions, benefits administration, employee recognition, and conflict resolution, maintaining a supportive, compassionate culture.

Performance Management: Coordinate annual evaluations, competency checks, and mandatory skill assessments. 



Qualifications

Experience: 1–3+ years of experience in HR, preferably within a healthcare, home health, or hospice environment.

Knowledge: Strong understanding of employment law and, ideally, Medicare/Medicaid hospice benefit regulations.

Skills: Excellent communication, organizational skills, proficiency in HRIS/electronic health records, and empathy. 



This role is critical in ensuring that staff are not only qualified but also properly trained to provide palliative care to patients and families during difficult times.

title: eCommerce Business Executive
description:
Own Category Growth, Optimize Performance, and Expand Market Dominance.



Hadley Designs is seeking a Business Executive to take full ownership of a multi-million dollar product category, with Amazon as the primary growth channel. This is a high-impact role for a results-driven e-commerce operator who thrives on ownership, accountability, and driving measurable growth through disciplined execution and data-backed decision-making.



While this role operates deeply within Amazon, we welcome candidates who have driven growth across e-commerce, DTC, or marketplace environments and are excited to apply that experience within a scaled Amazon ecosystem.



How You’ll Win at Hadley Designs:

We believe performance comes from strong systems executed consistently.



Every eCommerce Business Executive is expected to progress through three stages:



- Learn the Playbook – Understand our category strategy, operating standards, and decision frameworks

- Apply the Playbook – Execute accurately and consistently to deliver predictable, scalable results

- Enhance the Playbook – Improve systems with new ideas and strategies after mastery is demonstrated



This order matters. We value innovation, but only when it is grounded in proven execution. Our playbook exists to help high performers win faster, create leverage, and scale impact — not slow them down.



To formally apply, apply HERE:

title: Detail Detective – Video Transaction Reviewer
description:
Job Summary

The Transaction Auditor is responsible for accurately processing customer purchase transactions across all market locations. This role involves reviewing video footage to verify items taken, encoding items into the system with correct quantities, and ensuring that all daily transactions are completed with precision and consistency.



This position requires strong attention to detail, high levels of focus, patience for repetitive tasks, and a strong sense of accountability.



Key Responsibilities

- Review video footage to verify customer purchases.

- Identify items taken and accurately encode them into the system, including correct quantities.

- Process transactions efficiently, averaging 1 to 2 minutes per transaction.

- Ensure all transactions for the assigned shift or day are completed.

- Flag inconsistencies, discrepancies, and any other i
.

- Escalate and report irregularities, suspicious activity, or system issues promptly.

- Maintain high accuracy levels, especially when distinguishing between similar-looking items.

- Ensure consistent attendance and availability during assigned working hours.

- Follow established procedures and maintain confidentiality of footage and transaction data.





Qualifications

- High attention to detail and strong visual accuracy.

- Ability to maintain focus for extended periods.

- Patient and comfortable performing repetitive tasks throughout the day.

- Strong sense of responsibility and commitment to completing daily workload.

- Ability to detect inconsistencies and exercise sound judgment.

- Basic computer proficiency and ability to quickly learn transaction systems.

- Strong integrity and reliability.

- Good written and verbal communication skills for reporting issues.

- Must demonstrate consistent attendance and punctuality.





Preferred Attributes

- Experience in transaction processing, auditing, or retail operations is an advantage.

- Experience reviewing CCTV footage is a plus.

- Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

- Strong organizational and time management skills.



To apply and ensure your application is considered, do all of the following two steps:

1. DM with a quick introduction to yourself if you're interested. Include the word "cupcake" *in your subject line*, and Include your resume. ++Bonus points for a video introduction, though it's not strictly required

2. Fill out this form:

3. Add the Letters "TA" in the "What's your full name" field [E.g. TA - John Doe], so we know what role you are applying for.

title: Personal Billing Assistant / Senior Medical Billing Specialist
description:
Personal Billing Assistant / Senior Medical Billing Specialist



Location: Remote (International Candidates Welcome)

Employment Type: Independent Contractor

Schedule: Full-Time (Must overlap with U.S. PST business hours)

Reports To: Lead Manager – Billing Operations



About the Role



We are seeking an experienced Personal Billing Assistant / Senior Medical Billing Specialist to work directly with the Lead Manager and support the day-to-day billing operations for a behavioral health facility.



This role is ideal for someone who is highly experienced in U.S. medical billing, credentialing, insurance claims, and revenue cycle management. The successful candidate will take ownership of key billing processes, resolve claim issues, and help improve systems and workflows.



You will play a critical role in ensuring claims are processed efficiently and revenue is collected accurately while helping streamline operations.



Key Responsibilities

Credentialing & Contracting



Manage provider credentialing with commercial insurance companies, Medicare, and Medicaid



Maintain and update CAQH profiles and attestations



Monitor and follow up on pending credentialing applications



Ensure providers are successfully paneled with insurance payers



Revenue Cycle Management



Manage the full revenue cycle for a behavioral health client



Prepare and submit clean claims (electronic and paper)



Investigate and resolve claim denials



Identify the root cause of billing issues and implement solutions



Monitor claims and reimbursement status



Insurance Communication



Make outbound calls to insurance companies



Verify benefits and claim status



Appeal denied claims



Explain billing details such as:



Deductibles



Coinsurance



Out-of-pocket maximums



Strong spoken English is required as you will represent the billing team in calls with insurers.



Systems & Process Improvement



Document and develop Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for billing tasks



Identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements



Help improve billing processes and operational systems



Required Qualifications

Experience



Minimum 3+ years of experience in U.S. medical billing



Credentialing Expertise



Experience with:



CAQH management



Payer portals



Provider enrollment



Denial Management



Strong ability to analyze claim denials and resolve underlying issues



Insurance Knowledge



Understanding of:



Deductibles



Coinsurance



Out-of-pocket maximums



Communication



Excellent verbal and written English communication skills



Comfortable making insurance calls and handling complex conversations



Work Style



Highly organized and detail-oriented



Self-starter who can work independently



Strong problem-solving ability



Preferred Qualifications



Candidates with the following experience will stand out:



Experience working for a U.S. insurance company



CPC or CCS certification (AAPC or AHIMA)



Experience with behavioral or mental health billing



Strong interest in improving systems and operational efficiency
