title: Medical Executive Assistant
description:
Medical Executive Assistant



A growing physician practice is seeking a Medical Executive Assistant to provide a combination of healthcare administrative support, executive assistance, and operational coordination. This role will support day-to-day administrative functions of the practice while helping maintain efficient workflows and organized medical documentation.



Responsibilities include managing provider credentialing with hospitals and insurance companies, maintaining calendars, assisting with data entry, organizing sensitive medical records, and supporting EMR-related tasks.



This role may also assist with insurance-related administrative work, including prior authorizations and patient pre-operative coordination when needed. In addition, the position will help with operational and administrative projects such as conducting online research, building physician referral lists, compiling contact databases, and organizing information for outreach and follow-up activities.



The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced medical environment. This position requires someone who can bring structure to a busy practice, work independently, and take ownership of tasks from start to finish.



Core Responsibilities

• Manage provider credentialing and enrollment with hospitals and insurance companies

• Assist with insurance verification, authorizations, and payer communication

• Support patient pre-operative coordination when needed

• Retrieve, organize, and send medical documentation including procedure notes, consult notes, operative reports, visit notes, and imaging records

• Maintain calendar scheduling,
 coordination, and administrative support for the physician or practice leadership

• Perform data entry and maintain organization of sensitive medical and operational documentation

• Maintain tracking sheets, provider records, referral lists, and operational databases

• Conduct online research to compile physician referral lists, provider directories, and contact databases

• Organize information for outreach, operational tracking, and follow-up activities

• Support administrative and operational projects as needed



Requirements

• Prior experience in healthcare administration, medical administrative roles, credentialing, insurance verification, or medical executive assistant positions

• Familiarity with medical terminology and healthcare workflows

• Experience with provider credentialing and insurance enrollment processes

• Experience working with EMR/EHR systems

• Hands-on experience with insurance authorizations and payer communication

• Experience organizing and managing medical documentation

• Strong administrative skills including calendar management,
 coordination, and data entry

• Experience conducting online research and compiling structured information

• Highly organized and detail-oriented

• Strong communication skills

• Independent, resourceful, and proactive

• Strong follow-through and ability to manage multiple priorities

• Professional when communicating with hospitals, clinics, and insurance companies

• Strong problem-solving ability and ownership mindset

• Ability to work with minimal supervision

title: Senior Lead Generation And Email Marketing Specialist
description:
Description

Description

We are seeking a Senior Lead Generation Specialist who will be esponsible for designing, executing, and managing lead generation and referral development strategy. Oversees sourcing, research, outreach, qualification, and scheduling of pre-assessment appointments. Manages referral outreach campaigns to increase inbound and outbound referral opportunities. Supports up to the pre-assessment stage and, when needed, conducts pre-assessment calls to assist the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion. Ensures accurate documentation, disciplined follow-up, compliance, and strict protection of proprietary information.



Core Responsibilities: 



Develop and execute lead generation strategy to consistently produce warm, qualified prospects for intake and conversion.

Build and manage outreach campaigns targeting private-pay prospects and referral partners to increase lead flow and strengthen referral pipelines.

Source, research, organize, clean, segment, and manage prospect and referral lists for optimized outreach and conversion.

Conduct structured multi-channel outreach (calls, SMS,
) using approved messaging and compliance standards.

Qualify prospects based on service need, urgency, location, and suitability.

Nurture leads through structured follow-up cycles until ready for pre-assessment scheduling.

Schedule pre-assessment appointments directly with the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion and confirm details with clients.

When required, conduct pre-assessment calls to support the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion during highvolume or urgent periods.

Accurately update CRM stages, notes, tags, tasks, and communication logs immediately after every interaction.

Notify the Managing Member and Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion when leads become warm, preassessments are booked, or conversions occur.

Produce weekly lead generation and referral performance reports including lead volume, warm leads, campaign effectiveness, and recommendations for improvement.

Drive outbound and referral campaign performance to generate the lead volume necessary to support growth trajectory.

Continuously refine outreach cadence, messaging, targeting, and list quality based on performance metrics and conversion outcomes.

Collaborate with the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion to ensure smooth hand-offs and optimal lead readiness for pre-assessment.

Collaborate with the Supervisor for HR Recruitment to align staffing needs with projected lead and referral volume.

Maintain communication flow through WhatsApp channels and ensure all appointments are updated accurately on internal calendars.

Identify declines in lead quality, referral engagement, or outreach performance early and provide corrective strategies.



Data Protection & Confidentiality Requirements:



May only work with leads sourced personally or assigned for outreach; proprietary leads, lists, or strategies may not be exported, saved, copied, shared, reused, or retained outside approved systems.

Breach of confidentiality, improper handling of data, or unauthorized retention of contact information results in immediate termination.



Performance Standards:



Production of high-quality warm leads and referral opportunities aligned with intake and service needs. • Strong conversion rates from outreach to qualified warm status.

CRM documentation must be complete, accurate, and updated in real time.

Reliable scheduling of pre-assessment appointments with confirmed client communication.

Weekly reporting delivered on time with actionable insights and performance analysis.

Professional, compliant, and consistent communication across all outreach efforts.

Requirements

5+ years of experience in end-to-end lead generation, outreach campaigns, and appointment setting (High-ticket B2B or Healthcare/Home Care experience preferred).

Proven ability to manage the technical side of cold outreach, including domain procurement, DNS setup (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and inbox warming to ensure high deliverability.

Expert-level proficiency in for automated sequence management and lead nurturing.

Advanced skills in lead scraping and data mining to build high-quality, targeted prospect lists from scratch.

Ability to manage and scale operations to handle a high volume of leads daily without comproising data integrity or deliverability.

Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:



Internet Connection:

Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.

Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.

Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.

Primary Device:

Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:

Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.

A minimum of 8 GB RAM.

Backup Device:

Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.

Must be functional during power interruptions.

A functioning webcam.

A noise-canceling USB headset.

A quiet, dedicated home office space.

Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.

title: Drafter / Landscape Architect
description:
About the role

Calimingo Pools is a growing Southern California pool and outdoor living company. We design and build high-end custom pools, spas, and landscape environments — and we need a sharp, dependable Drafter / Landscape Architect to help us keep projects moving smoothly from design to approved permit.



This is a fully remote position open to candidates based in the Philippines. You'll work closely with our US-based designers and engineers, preparing technical drawings and permit packages, communicating with city building departments, and helping manage the full permit lifecycle for multiple projects at a time. We value reliability, strong communication, and someone who takes ownership of their work.



What you'll do

Prepare detailed CAD drawings — site plans, section drawings, structural details, and full working plan sets ready for permit submittal.

Coordinate with our US-based designers and structural engineers to gather all information needed to complete permit packages and HOA submittals.

Convert raw job-site measurements into clean, accurate blank site plans for the design team to work from.

Research city planning and zoning requirements (setbacks, easements, code requirements) to ensure designs are buildable before work begins.

Communicate with California city building departments and HOA offices (via
, phone, and online portals) to expedite permit approvals.

Log and update project progress in our project management system, keeping clients and office staff consistently informed across multiple active projects.

Manage revision cycles and respond to plan check corrections from city departments until permits are approved.



Required skills

Proven experience as a Drafter, Landscape Architect, or similar role

Strong hands-on CAD software skills (AutoCAD, SketchUp, or similar)

Experience with 3D modeling tools

Familiarity with US city building departments and HOA submittal processes

Strong written and spoken English communication skills

Highly organized, self-directed, and able to manage multiple projects independently

Reliable internet connection and a dedicated home workspace



Nice to have

Background in pool, spa, or outdoor living design

Previous remote work experience with a US-based client or employer

Artistic eye or rendering ability for presentation drawings

Familiarity with project management tools (Buildertrend, Asana, or similar)

Degree in Landscape Architecture, Architecture, or Civil/Environmental Design

Experience reading and interpreting structural and civil engineering plans



What reliability looks like to us

We're not looking for someone to clock in and disappear. Our ideal candidate is someone who:

Responds promptly

Replies to messages within a reasonable window during agreed working hours

Meets deadlines

Delivers drawings on time and flags issues early — not at the last minute

Takes ownership

Tracks their own tasks without needing to be chased, and follows through

Communicates clearly

Keeps the team updated, asks smart questions, and escalates when needed



What to expect

Overlap with US Pacific Time (PST/PDT) is required — at least 4–5 hours of overlap per day

All communication and coordination is done remotely via
, video calls, and our project management platform

You'll work directly with the project lead, designers, and engineers — a small, collaborative team

Volume varies by season — strong project flow with opportunities to grow as the company scale



How to apply

Send us the following with the subject line "Drafter / Landscape Architect – [Your Name]"



1.Your updated CV / resume

2.Portfolio or work samples (CAD drawings, site plans, or similar)

3.A short note (3–5 sentences) on your remote work experience and why this role interests you



Applications with incomplete attachments will not be considered. We review every submission carefully.

title: Real Estate Research & Due Diligence Operator (Remote)
description:
Company: Down To Earth Investments LLC

Location: Remote

Schedule: Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST

Pay: $9/hour + $500 Finder’s Fee for Deals You Find



About Us:

Down To Earth Investments LLC is a U.S.-based land investment company. We acquire land and increase its value by subdividing properties and creating new opportunities for development.

Our business runs on great research and smart decisions. Before we purchase any land, we verify zoning, flood zones, county rules, and whether the property can legally be subdivided.

We are looking for someone who wants to be more than just a task worker.

We want someone who thinks like a business operator — someone who takes ownership of their work, investigates problems, and helps the team make better decisions.





Our Culture:

One thing we love about our team is that everyone operates like a business owner.

Our employees take responsibility for their work and treat the business like it’s their own. They solve problems, figure things out, and take initiative.

If you prefer being told every small step of what to do, this role probably isn’t a good fit.

But if you enjoy thinking, researching, and taking ownership, you will do very well here.





Your Role:

Your main job is to research land properties and verify whether they are good investment opportunities.

You will investigate properties, gather critical information, and report your findings so our acquisitions team can make smart buying decisions. This role is a mix of research, investigation, and communication.





Responsibilities:



Property Due Diligence

Research and verify important property details such as:

Lot size and boundaries

Zoning classification

Minimum lot size requirements

County and township rules

Flood zone status

Road access

Utility availability (when possible)



You will use tools such as:

LandPortal

Land ID

County and township websites

GIS maps





Your job is to organize the information clearly so we can quickly understand if a deal works or not.





County Research & Calls

Sometimes the only way to confirm information is by contacting the county directly.

You may need to call planning or zoning departments to confirm things like:

Subdivision requirements

Minimum lot sizes

Zoning rules

Development restrictions





Because of this, strong spoken English is required.



Requirements



Excellent English communication skills

Comfortable calling U.S. county offices

Strong online research ability

Detail-oriented and organized

Able to work 9 AM – 5 PM EST

Reliable internet connection





Experience with real estate, land research, GIS tools, or due diligence is a plus but not required.





Compensation

$9 per hour



How to Apply

Please include the following in your application:

A short introduction about yourself

Any experience with research or real estate

Confirmation you can work 9 AM – 5 PM EST

Your internet speed

Answer this question:



If you were researching whether a piece of land can be subdivided, what are the first 3 things you would check?

We’re looking for someone who enjoys figuring things out and taking ownership, not just completing tasks.

If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: A-Team WE - Brand & Paid Media Manager

title: Book Keeper / Accounts Receivables
description: A bookkeeper is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records for a company or organization. Their duties include recording financial transactions, updating statements, and ensuring accuracy in accounts payable and receivable. They prepare financial reports, balance sheets, and profit and loss statements, and may handle payroll and bank reconciliations. Attention to detail, proficiency in accounting software, and a solid understanding of bookkeeping principles are essential for this role. Effective communication and organizational skills are also important, as bookkeepers often work closely with accountants and other financial professionals.

title: Real Estate Data Research VA
description:
Please review this job post completely before applying.



Job Title: Real Estate Research & Data Analyst (Virtual Assistant)

Type: Full-Time, Long-Term

Hours: 40 hrs/week (EST)

Location: Remote

Start Date: Immediate (within 7 days)



About the Role

description:

About the Role



We are a growing digital productivity and workflow consulting company helping businesses streamline operations, automate processes, and improve team productivity using modern work management systems.



Our clients are primarily companies based in the United States and Australia.

Your main goal is to generate high-quality sales opportunities for our closing team.

, and other channels

title: Facebook Media Buyer + GoHighLevel Specialist (Long Term Position)
description:
ABOUT ROOFIGNITE

RoofIgnite is a performance marketing agency based in Toronto, Canada. We help residential roofing contractors across the United States generate qualified homeowner leads through
 Ads and a lead qualification system.



We’re growing fast and need a dedicated Media Buyer to help us manage campaigns and keep things running smoothly.



THE ROLE

You’ll be handling the technical setup of
 campaigns and 
 
 for our roofing clients. You’ll also manage ongoing actions to ensure clients are successful based on performance data and KPIs. We have weekly meetings to review what’s working and what needs improvement.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:



Setting Up New Campaigns

-Create
 campaigns in Meta Ads Manager (structure, targeting, budgets)

-Build GoHighLevel funnels, landing pages, and automations (templates provided)

-Set up lead tracking, forms, and ensure pixels are firing correctly

-Handle A2P 10DLC setup (SMS registration and number transfers) and technical integrations

-Make sure all systems are connected so leads flow correctly



Managing Existing Campaigns

-Check performance daily/weekly (CPM, CPC, CTR, cost per lead, booking rates)

-Flag underperforming ad sets and campaigns

-Deploy new creatives when ready

-Fix technical issues (pixel issues, automation failures, tracking problems)

-Keep accounts organized and documented



Weekly Meetings & Reporting

-Pull weekly data and organize it clearly

-Join weekly meetings to discuss account status and blockers

-Share honest feedback and improvement ideas

-Respond quickly to urgent issues

-When something breaks, you fix it or clearly explain what’s wrong



WHO YOU ARE:



Experience You Need

-1–2+ years of hands-on experience with
/Meta Ads

-Strong GoHighLevel experience (funnels, automations, landing pages)

-Comfortable with technical setups: pixels, UTMs, API connections

-You understand CTR, CPM, CPC, cost per lead, and booking rates

-Experience with A2P setup is a plus



How You Work

-Detail-oriented and able to follow processes exactly

-Fast learner who takes feedback well

-Proactive — you don’t wait to be told what to do

-Clear English communication (written and spoken)

-Reliable and looking for long-term work

-Available during EST business hours



Nice to Have

-Experience with Zapier or project management tools

-Basic Canva skills

-Experience in roofing, HVAC, or home services

-Familiarity with call recording tools (Fathom, etc.)

-Ability to analyze data, create action plans, and execute



WORKING ENVIRONMENT

Location: Fully Remote

-Type: Full-Time, Long-Term

-Hours: 40–40+ hours per week, Monday–Friday, 9AM–6PM EST

-Flexible Saturday Availability as needed (Occasional Saturday morning meetings (weekly if needed))

-Team: Media buying and operations team

-Meetings: Weekly check-ins + occasional strategy calls



WHAT WE OFFER

-Salary: $900–$1,300 USD/month (net, based on experience)

-Performance Bonuses based on account results

-Growth opportunities as the company scales

-Long-term stability — we are building a real team



APPLICATION PROCESS



To apply, please provide:



-Speed Test Result: Run a test at https://www.speedtest.net

 and share the link or paste the result

-60-Second Video Introduction (required): Record a short video using 
 or 
 and answer:

--Your name and location

--Your experience with
 Ads and 
 (specific examples)

--Why you want to work with RoofIgnite

--One technical problem you’ve solved in Meta Ads or GoHighLevel



Optional but Helpful

-Screenshots of GoHighLevel funnels you’ve built


 ad campaigns you’ve managed (results if available)

-Any workflows or automations you’ve created



Confirmation

-Include the phrase “I SAW THE FULL POST” in your application



WHY JOIN ROOFIGNITE

-We’re growing and scaling fast

-You’ll have real ownership and responsibility

-You’ll learn how real marketing operations are run

-Non-corporate, energetic, ambitious team

-Clear growth path as we hire more people

-Transparent leadership and expectations



NEXT STEPS

-Submit your full application

-We’ll review and reach out to top candidates within 48 hours

-First call: Quick screening

-Start Date: ASAP



RoofIgnite is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Medical VA (Operations Assistant)
description:
We are seeking a Virtual Medical Assistant (VMA) to oversee the overall operational health of the client’s clinic. This role combines medical knowledge, administrative precision, and leadership skills to ensure smooth workflows, accurate documentation, and proactive team coordination.

Key Responsibilities

Review Explanation of Benefits (EOBs): Ensure accuracy in billing and insurance documentation

SOAP Notes: Monitor compliance and accuracy, ensuring timely and complete documentation.

Appointments: Track missed and canceled appointments

Reports: Generate and distribute timely reports on outstanding balances and other operational metrics with insights and recommendations

Team Coordination: Facilitate seamless communication across staff and providers

Required Soft Skills

Proactivity: Anticipate scheduling conflicts, outstanding balance, and patient needs before they escalate.

Attention to Detail: Ensure completion and accuracy in SOAP notes, EOB reviews, and financial reports.

Executive Presence: Communicate with confidence and professionalism when coordinating with providers and tea
mbers.

Anticipating Needs: Identify potential gaps in clinic operations and propose solutions to improve efficiency.

Critical Thinking: Analyze appointment trends, billing issues, and workflow challenges to recommend actionable improvements.

Mandatory Requirements

Minimum of 1 year experience as a Medical Virtual Assistant.

Exposure to CPT and ICD?10 codes.

Proven leadership experience

MAC OS
